title: Bakery Website with Menu, Admin Panel, and Rewards Program

requirements.text: Design a warm, artisan bakery visual theme with a color palette inspired by baked goods (creams, warm tans, deep browns, soft terracotta), handcrafted-style typography, and a cozy layout. Apply the theme consistently across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages share a consistent color palette of warm bakery tones (creams, tans, browns, terracotta)
Typography evokes a handcrafted, artisan feel
Layout feels cozy and inviting throughout

requirements.text: Create a public-facing bakery homepage with a hero section, a navigation bar linking to the Menu and Rewards pages, and a brief bakery description.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage displays a hero section with bakery name and tagline
Navigation links to Menu and Rewards pages
Brief bakery description is visible on the homepage

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model for menu items. Each item must have: a unique ID, name, description, price, category (e.g., breads, pastries, cakes, drinks), available status, and an image URL field.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu item record includes id, name, description, price, category, isAvailable, and imageUrl fields
Items can be created, read, updated, and deleted via backend functions
Items are persisted in stable storage

requirements.text: Build a public Menu page that displays all available menu items grouped by category. Each item shows its name, description, and price.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu page is accessible at a /menu route
Items are grouped by category with clear section headers
Each item card shows name, description, and formatted price
Only items marked as available are shown to the public

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity-based admin authentication. A designated admin principal (configurable in the backend) gains access to an admin panel. Non-admin users see only the public site.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin logs in via Internet Identity
Backend validates the caller's principal against a stored admin principal
Admin panel route is inaccessible to non-admin users
Admin can be set as the canister deployer's principal by default

requirements.text: Build an Admin Panel page where the admin can add new menu items, edit existing items (name, description, price, category, availability, image URL), and remove items from the menu. Changes take effect immediately on the public menu.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel lists all menu items with edit and delete buttons
Admin can add a new item via a form with all required fields
Admin can edit any field of an existing item
Admin can toggle an item's availability
Admin can delete an item
All changes are persisted in the backend and reflected immediately on the public menu

requirements.text: Implement a backend rewards program. Track each customer (by Internet Identity principal) and the number of items they have purchased. Every 5 items purchased earns 1 free treat reward. Store: customer principal, total items purchased, redeemable free treats count.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer rewards record includes principal, totalItemsPurchased, and freeTreeatsAvailable fields
Backend function to record a purchase increments totalItemsPurchased
For every 5 items purchased, freeTreeatsAvailable increments by 1
Backend function to redeem a free treat decrements freeTreeatsAvailable (minimum 0)
All rewards data is persisted in stable storage

requirements.text: Build a public Rewards page where logged-in customers can view their rewards status: total items purchased, number of free treats available, and how many more items until the next free treat. Include a button to log in with Internet Identity if not authenticated.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewards page is accessible at a /rewards route
Unauthenticated users see a prompt to log in with Internet Identity
Authenticated users see their total items purchased count
Authenticated users see how many free treats they have available
A progress indicator shows how many more items are needed to earn the next free treat (e.g., 3 more items to go)

requirements.text: Add an admin section in the Admin Panel to configure the rewards program rule: the number of items required per free treat (default: 5). Also allow the admin to look up any customer by principal and manually adjust their rewards balance.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can view and update the 'items per free treat' threshold (default 5)
Changes to the threshold apply to future reward calculations
Admin can search a customer by principal and view their rewards record
Admin can manually add or subtract free treats for a customer

requirements.text: Add a simple 'Record Purchase' flow for the admin panel: the admin can select a customer principal and the number of items purchased in a transaction, and submit it to update the customer's rewards balance.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel has a 'Record Purchase' form with a principal input and item count input
Submitting the form calls the backend to update that customer's rewards
Success confirmation is shown after recording a purchase

requirements.text: Generate and use a set of static bakery-themed images: a hero banner and a logo/icon for the site header.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero banner image is displayed on the homepage hero section
Logo/icon appears in the navigation bar
Images are served from frontend/public/assets/generated

constraints: Admin authentication must use Internet Identity only — no external auth providers
All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Customer self-registration outside of Internet Identity login
Real-time inventory tracking or stock counts
Multiple admin roles or granular permissions

required: Warm artisan bakery hero banner with freshly baked breads, pastries, and golden light atmosphere, rustic wooden table surface (bakery-hero-banner.dim_1400x500.png)
Circular bakery logo illustration featuring a wheat stalk and a small croissant, warm brown and cream tones (bakery-logo.dim_128x128.png)
